title: ‘Takeovers’ at Hampton Beach in N.H. lead to multiple fights and arrests
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Hampton Beach in N.H. saw multiple fights and arrests during planned 'takeover' events on Tuesday.
Police made 50 arrests, including charges for rioting, alcohol possession, and DUIs at Hampton Beach.
Authorities had prepared crowd control teams and maintained patrols due to the advertised events.

title: Amtrak is expanding Berkshire Flyer service this summer
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Amtrak's Berkshire Flyer service will expand with a second round trip starting June 12, 2026.
The service runs Fridays and Sundays from Pittsfield, MA to NYC's Moynihan Train Hall through Sept. 19.
The Berkshire Flyer stops include Yonkers, Croton-Harmon, Poughkeepsie, Rhinecliff, Hudson, and Albany-Rensselaer.

title: Cambridge City Council votes to end use of ShotSpotter technology
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Cambridge City Council voted to end the use of ShotSpotter technology, a gunshot detection system, within 90 days.
Councilors expressed concerns over ShotSpotter's reliability and its impact on over-policing in communities of color.
ShotSpotter's termination follows debates and criticism over its effectiveness and potential privacy risks in Cambridge.

title: Grand jury indicts Boston police officer on charge of manslaughter in fatal shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A grand jury indicted Boston police officer Nicholas O'Malley for voluntary manslaughter in Stephenson King Jr.'s death.
O'Malley allegedly shot King during a carjacking incident in Roxbury; bodycam footage shows King trying to flee.
O'Malley is on paid leave; his arraignment is on June 4; civil rights lawyer Ben Crump supports King's family.

title: How hot are Boston classrooms today? See temperatures reported across BPS schools.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Charlestown High School recorded the highest classroom temperature at 84.59 degrees on May 20, 2026.
Boston schools averaged an indoor classroom temperature of 76.49 degrees, according to a district analysis.
Mattapan’s Young Achiever’s K-8 school recorded the coolest classroom temperature at 70.05 degrees.

title: ‘I stink at fantasy football’: Fan at Red Sox game goes viral with creative punishment
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: A Red Sox game attendee went viral with an 'I stink at fantasy football' sign at Kauffman Stadium.
The fan's appearance was a light-hearted punishment for finishing last in a fantasy football league.
The Red Sox defeated the Kansas City Royals 7-1 during the game where the fan was spotted.

title: Rosebud in Somerville is closed, but just for renovations
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Rosebud Bar and Kitchen in Somerville is temporarily closed for renovations until mid-June, says owner Koushik Koganti.
Renovations include new flooring, an updated HVAC system, and a revised menu with American diner and Indian cuisine.
The historic dining car, under Bindaas Restaurant Group since 2023, was temporarily closed before for similar upgrades.

title: ‘Infuriation’: Animal control reports rescuing puppy from 100-degree car at Legacy Place
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 5-month-old husky was rescued by Dedham Animal Control from a 100-degree car at Legacy Place in Dedham.
The puppy was left in the vehicle while its owners were shopping; Legacy Place security alerted animal control.
Dedham Animal Control praised security staff and emphasized the importance of reporting such incidents to save lives.

title: Widow of college police officer killed in crash sues Boston music venue, alleges they overserved other driver
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Widow of Sgt. Jeremy Cole sues House of Blues Boston for allegedly overserving driver Keoma Duarte before fatal 2024 crash.
Keoma Duarte, driver involved in the crash, pleaded not guilty to manslaughter charges; his blood alcohol level was allegedly 0.19.
Laura Cole seeks over $50 million in damages, citing wrongful death and negligence by House of Blues Boston in lawsuit.

title: 3 must-see summer movies coming soon to a theater near you
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Steven Spielberg directs 'Disclosure Day,' an alien film premiering on June 12, 2026.
Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey,' starring Matt Damon, releases on July 17, 2026.
The Anthony Bourdain biopic 'Tony,' filmed in Massachusetts, will premiere in August 2026.
